deep_dive.title: KD-NVC: A Search-and-Distill Framework to Accelerate Neural Video Coding
deep_dive.abstract: While neural video coding (NVC) has achieved remarkable rate-distortion performance, real-time decoding on edge devices has become an important demand but remains limited by high complexity. Knowledge distillation (KD) is widely used for model acceleration, yet its application to NVC faces critical challenges. Specifically, the heterogeneity of NVC sub-modules renders uniform architectural reduction suboptimal, necessitating a per-module design for better rate-distortion-speed trade-off. However, searching for diverse architectures via existing neural architecture search (NAS) algorithms is unaffordable due to the expensive training cost of neural video codecs. Moreover, after the lightweight architecture is determined, existing distillation methods overlook the feature-energy sparsity induced by the rate-constraint, which is essential for maintaining compression performance. To address these issues, we propose a two-stage distillation framework KD-NVC. In the first stage, we introduce an acceleration-efficiency-based neural architecture search (AE-NAS) algorithm. It explores the module-wise Pareto frontier to adaptively allocate the acceleration budget across heterogeneous modules. Also, it introduces the acceleration-efficiency metric to determine the final student architecture without practically training all architecture-level candidates. In the second stage, we design an energy-aware feature distillation (EFD) loss that aligns the spatially-aggregated feature-energy signatures between the teacher and student codecs, transferring the rate-induced sparsity patterns for better compression efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently outperforms existing codec-oriented distillation methods, and achieves 69 FPS decoding at 1080p on RTX 5060 while maintaining comparable RD performance to VTM-LDB.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: search-and-distill knowledge distillation for neural video coding acceleration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 搜索与蒸馏框架加速神经视频编码
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 搜索与蒸馏框架加速神经视频编码
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 搜索与蒸馏框架加速神经视频编码
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes KD-NVC, a search-and-distill framework that uses neural architecture search tailored to each NVC sub-module and a motion-aware distillation loss to accelerate neural video codecs while maintaining rate-distortion performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 神经视频编码（NVC）在边缘设备上难以实时解码，现有知识蒸馏方法未考虑子模块异构性且架构搜索成本高昂。本文提出KD-NVC框架，针对NVC各子模块进行个性化架构搜索，并设计运动感知蒸馏损失以保持编码质量。实验表明该方法在加速解码的同时几乎不损失率失真性能，为低复杂度视频编码提供了有效训练技巧。

deep_dive.title: Few-step Generative Models as Lossy Compression
deep_dive.abstract: DiffC provides a principled way to reuse pre-trained diffusion models for lossy compression, but its encoding and decoding procedures remain slow because they require many discretized forward and reverse steps. We study whether few-step generative models -- Rectified Flow, Consistency Trajectory Models (CTM), and MeanFlow -- can be cast as codecs within the same reverse channel coding (RCC) framework. The main challenge is that RCC requires posterior and shared distribution parameters, whereas these models do not explicitly parameterize intermediate conditional distributions. For Rectified Flow and MeanFlow, we use the equivalence between velocity parameterization and diffusion-style denoising parameterization to derive the quantities required by RCC. For CTM, which is distilled from EDM, we adopt the EDM noise parameterization together with local Gaussian approximations of the sender and shared distributions at intermediate states. This yields a proof-of-concept probabilistic formulation that enables compression with pre-trained few-step generative models without retraining. On low-resolution benchmarks, the resulting codecs reduce encoding and decoding time and improve realism in the low-bit-rate regime.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: casts few-step generative models as lossy compression codecs under reverse channel coding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将少步生成模型作为有损压缩编解码器
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将少步生成模型作为有损压缩编解码器
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将少步生成模型作为有损压缩编解码器
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Explores using Rectified Flow, Consistency Trajectory Models, and MeanFlow as lossy compressors within the reverse channel coding framework, achieving faster encoding/decoding than DiffC.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: DiffC利用预训练扩散模型进行有损压缩但编解码缓慢。本文研究少步生成模型（Rectified Flow、CTM、MeanFlow）是否能在逆信道编码框架中充当编解码器。通过速度参数化与扩散去噪参数化的等价推导，成功将部分模型转化为压缩码字，加速了编解码过程并保持压缩质量。为生成式视频压缩提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: AAD-1: Asymmetric Adversarial Distillation for One-Step Autoregressive Video Generation
quick_skim.abstract: We present AAD-1, an Asymmetric Adversarial Distillation framework for One-step autoregressive image-to-video generation. State-of-the-art methods adopt adversarial distillation but suffer from motion collapse and training instability, resulting in static videos. AAD-1 addresses these challenges through two key designs in architecture and training strategy. Our key architectural insight is to break the symmetry between generator and discriminator. While the generator remains causal to preserve autoregressive sampling capability, the discriminator attends bidirectionally over the full spatiotemporal context and produces a single holistic realism score for the entire video sequence. This asymmetric design enables the discriminator to effectively detect global temporal failures and long-range drift that cause motion collapse in autoregressive generation. To stabilize training, we introduce a phased strategy that first uses distribution matching to bootstrap a stable one-step generator, providing a warm-up phase that brings the student distribution closer to the teacher before adversarial distillation begins. Extensive experiments on VBench demonstrate that AAD-1 achieves state-of-the-art performance in one-step autoregressive video generation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: asymmetric adversarial distillation for one-step autoregressive video generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 非对称对抗蒸馏用于一步自回归视频生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 非对称对抗蒸馏用于一步自回归视频生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 非对称对抗蒸馏用于一步自回归视频生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AAD-1 uses asymmetric adversarial distillation for one-step autoregressive video generation, breaking symmetry between generator and discriminator to avoid motion collapse and enable efficient training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对一步自回归视频生成中的运动崩溃和训练不稳定问题，提出非对称对抗蒸馏框架AAD-1。该方法使生成器保持自回归因果性，判别器则对整个视频序列进行双向全局判断，从而有效提升生成质量和训练稳定性。实验表明该方法能生成高质量动态视频。贡献在于提供了高效视频生成的新范式，对生成式视频压缩有借鉴意义。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 调研扩散模型、生成式先验与视频编解码融合方案，挖掘感知质量、语义编码、生成式预测的创新研究点。调研深度学习运动估计、光流、运动补偿与编解码联合训练策略，总结帧间预测模块的网络设计与优化方法。, especially work that connects or combines: Scalable NVC; Joint learning of motion estimation and compression for video codec; Latent diffusion; Generative video compression; end-to-end learned video compression frameworks; inter-frame prediction network design and optimization; Neural motion compensation module in end to end video compression; Perceptual rate distortion optimization for generative video coding; Diffusion model based end to end video compression method.

quick_skim.title: Knowledge Distillation for Visual Autoregressive Models
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive (AR) image generation models are highly expressive but computationally intensive, motivating effective model compression. Knowledge distillation (KD) is a natural approach for model compression and has been widely studied in language modeling, yet its behavior in visual AR generation remains underexplored. In this work, we present the first systematic study of distillation strategies for AR image models. Our analysis shows that while standard distillation can yield meaningful gains, recent methods developed for language do not directly transfer to images: long decoding horizons and visual token ambiguity make teacher supervision unreliable especially under student-conditioned contexts. To address this, we propose VarKD, a distillation framework for visual autoregressive models that distills on student samples while selectively applying teacher supervision and reducing token-level ambiguity. Experiments on ImageNet across multiple AR backbones show that VarKD consistently outperforms prior distillation baselines, narrowing the gap to large-scale models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Knowledge distillation for visual autoregressive models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向视觉自回归模型的知识蒸馏
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向视觉自回归模型的知识蒸馏
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向视觉自回归模型的知识蒸馏
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: VarKD proposes a distillation framework for autoregressive image models, addressing challenges like long decoding horizons and visual token ambiguity through a student-conditioned refined teacher.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文首次系统研究了视觉自回归模型的知识蒸馏策略。发现标准蒸馏方法在图像生成中存在长解码视野和视觉令牌歧义问题，导致教师监督不可靠。为此提出VarKD框架，通过学生条件精炼教师来提供更可靠的监督信号。实验表明该方法能有效压缩模型且保持生成质量，为视频压缩模型的知识蒸馏提供了可迁移的方法论。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文首次系统研究了视觉自回归模型的知识蒸馏策略。发现标准蒸馏方法在图像生成中存在长解码视野和视觉令牌歧义问题，导致教师监督不可靠。为此提出VarKD框架，通过学生条件精炼教师来提供更可靠的监督信号。实验表明该方法能有效压缩模型且保持生成质量，为视频压缩模型的知识蒸馏提供了可迁移的方法论。

quick_skim.title: MotionEnhancer: Leveraging Video Diffusion for Motion-Enhanced Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: The new era has witnessed a remarkable capability to extend Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for tackling tasks of video understanding. While current VLMs excel at event- or story-level understanding, their ability to capture fine-grained motion details remains limited, primarily due to their focus on high-level static semantic structures and macro-event logic. In contrast, Video Diffusion Models (VDMs) are adept at modeling dynamic motion patterns, benefiting from large-scale video data and the intrinsic requirement of temporal generation. In this paper, we introduce MotionEnhancer, a novel approach that leverages motion priors distilled from a powerful video diffusion model as auxiliary supervision to enhance the motion understanding capability of a VLM via attention alignment. MotionEnhancer comprises two simple parameter-free modules, Motion-sensitive Head Selection (MHS) and Motion-salient Text Token Identification (MTTI), to directly extract and optimize motion-related attentions from the VDM in a computation-only manner. MotionEnhancer provides a scalable solution for motion understanding without additional training parameters, modifications to existing architectures, or tool calling. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MotionEnhancer can achieve consistent improvements over state-of-the-art VLMs on two motion-level video understanding benchmarks, especially on motion-related metrics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses video diffusion prior for motion, applicable to generative video compression
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用视频扩散先验增强运动，可迁移至生成式视频压缩中的运动估计与预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用视频扩散先验增强运动，可迁移至生成式视频压缩中的运动估计与预测

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MotionEnhancer leverages motion priors from a video diffusion model as auxiliary supervision to improve fine-grained motion understanding in VLMs, demonstrating transferable methods for generative video compression.

quick_skim.title: RAPID: Layer-Wise Redundancy-Aware Pruning and Importance-Driven Token Merging for Efficient ViT
quick_skim.abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) achieve strong performance but suffer from high computational costs due to quadratic self-attention complexity. Although token reduction techniques such as pruning and merging mitigate this, they typically overlook how representations evolve across network depth. We propose RAPID, a depth-aware token reduction framework that adapts reduction strategies to the layer-wise characteristics of token representations. The primary methodological contribution is a bifurcated strategy: in shallow-to-middle layers, RAPID employs a redundancy-similarity aware pruning metric to eliminate over-represented local patterns. As features transition to global semantic concepts in deeper layers, the framework shifts to an importance-similarity aware merging mechanism. This stage leverages classification (CLS) token attention weights to protect semantically critical tokens while fusing less important but similar neighbors. Empirical validation on ImageNet-1K using ViT and DeiT architectures demonstrates that RAPID establishes a superior accuracy-compression Pareto frontier compared to plug-and-play baselines such as ToMe and ToFu. RAPID is particularly robust in aggressive compression regimes, achieving up to 4.29% higher accuracy than ToMe at extreme reduction rates. Our framework provides a training-free template for optimizing vision models by aligning reduction strategies with hierarchical feature evolution.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Token pruning and merging for efficient ViT, applicable to video codec acceleration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 令牌剪枝与合并方法，可加速视频编解码

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 令牌剪枝与合并方法，可加速视频编解码
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes RAPID, a depth-aware token reduction framework that adapts pruning/merging strategies based on layer characteristics, reducing computation in ViTs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉Transformer因自注意力二次复杂度计算代价高，现有令牌缩减方法未考虑层间表示演化。本文提出RAPID深度感知框架：在浅中层使用冗余相似性剪枝消除局部模式，在深层转换为重要性驱动的令牌合并。该方法有效降低ViT计算量，其剪枝合并策略可启发视频编解码中轻量化模块的设计。

quick_skim.title: Beyond Skeletons: Learning Animation Directly from Driving Videos with Same2X Training Strategy
quick_skim.abstract: Human image animation aims to generate a video from a static reference image, guided by pose information extracted from a driving video. Existing approaches often rely on pose estimators to extract intermediate representations, but such signals are prone to errors under occlusion or complex poses. Building on these observations, we present DirectAnimator, a framework that bypasses pose extraction and directly learns from raw driving videos. We introduce a Driving Cue Triplet consisting of pose, face, and location cues that captures motion, expression, and alignment in a semantically rich yet stable form, and we fuse them through a CueFusion DiT block for reliable control during denoising. To make learning dependable when the driving and reference identities differ, we devise a Same2X training strategy that aligns cross-ID features with those learned from same-ID data, regularizing optimization and accelerating convergence. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DirectAnimator attains state-of-the-art visual quality and identity preservation while remaining robust to occlusions and complex articulation, and it does so with fewer computational resources. Our project page is at https://directanimator.github.io/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: latent diffusion for video generation with motion cues
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用扩散模型进行视频生成并融合运动线索

quick_skim.title: MilliVid: Hierarchical Latents for Long-Range Consistency in Video Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Video generative models have become increasingly powerful, but long-range consistency remains challenging to achieve because even a few dozen frames require impractically long transformer sequence lengths. We show that this issue can be mitigated by generating video using coarse-to-fine rollout within a multi-scale token space. Our approach is simple: first, we pre-train an autoencoder that compresses each frame into a hierarchy of tokens, with levels ranging from the typical latent resolution to only a handful of tokens per frame. The coarsest levels capture the most consequential information, such as scene layout and semantics, while finer levels add high-frequency appearance and texture. Then, we train a video diffusion model to generate these tokens using coarse-to-fine rollout. By carefully controlling the level of detail at which frames are generated and used as context during each rollout step, we are able to preserve long-range consistency in geometry and object permanence while spending less compute on the long-range consistency of less perceptually relevant details. We validate this approach using a custom dataset of long Minecraft videos, where it produces substantially more consistent rollouts compared to existing baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 层次化隐空间视频扩散模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 层次化隐空间视频扩散模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 层次化隐空间视频扩散模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MilliVid uses a hierarchical autoencoder to compress frames into multi-scale tokens and trains a video diffusion model for long-range consistent video generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对视频生成中长程一致性的挑战，提出MilliVid方法。首先训练一个层次化自编码器，将每一帧压缩成从粗到细的多尺度令牌序列，然后训练视频扩散模型在这些令牌空间中进行粗到细的生成。实验表明该方法能有效保持视频场景布局和语义的长期一致性，同时生成高频率细节。该方法为生成式视频编码提供了新的潜在表示思路。

quick_skim.title: Understanding Quantization-Aware Training: Gradients at Quantized Weights Bias to the Low-Loss Basin
quick_skim.abstract: Post-training quantization (PTQ) converts a trained full-precision model into low-bit weights without task-level retraining, while quantization-aware training (QAT) incorporates quantization into the training loop. Although PTQ is efficient and often accurate at moderate bitwidths, it can fail sharply at aggressive bitwidths; QAT is more expensive but can often recover the lost accuracy. We propose a unified geometric framework that explains both PTQ failure and QAT recovery. We model full-precision training as following a low-loss \emph{river} inside a wider \emph{valley}: a normal neighborhood of the river forms a nearly flat \emph{basin}, while leaving this basin incurs a sharp loss increase. When the quantization grid is comparable to the basin width, local PTQ objectives, including rounding and Hessian-based second-order reconstruction, can select a high-loss deployed quantized point outside the basin even when nearby low-loss quantized points exist. In this regime, straight-through-estimator-based QAT has a useful bias: it evaluates gradients at the deployed quantized weights while updating latent full-precision weights, causing the gradient to sense the valley wall and acquire an inward component that steers subsequent quantized iterates back into the basin. We formalize this mechanism through a local landscape model, construct a geometric PTQ failure mode, and prove finite-time QAT recovery under local quantizer-compatibility assumptions. Experiments across vision and language models under multiple neural-network quantization schemes corroborate the predicted basin-crossing failure of PTQ and the corresponding recovery mechanism of QAT.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: explains why QAT works and provides geometric framework, applicable to video codec QAT
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 揭示了量化感知训练的原理，为视频编码模型的QAT提供理论指导
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 揭示了量化感知训练的原理，为视频编码模型的QAT提供理论指导
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 揭示了量化感知训练的原理，为视频编码模型的QAT提供理论指导
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper provides a unified geometric framework explaining post-training quantization failure and quantization-aware training recovery, offering insights for training low-bit neural video codecs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 后训练量化在低位宽下性能急剧下降，而量化感知训练能恢复精度，但其内在原理尚不明确。本文提出统一的几何框架，将全精度训练建模为沿低损失河流的河谷，量化网格偏移导致损失陡增，而QAT通过梯度偏向低损失盆地实现恢复。该理论在多种模型和数据集上得到验证，为神经视频编码器的低位宽训练提供了理论依据和优化方向。

quick_skim.title: Latent Spatial Memory for Video World Models
quick_skim.abstract: Video world models that maintain 3D spatial consistency across generated frames typically rely on explicit point cloud memory constructed in RGB space. This design is both computationally expensive, requiring repeated rendering and VAE encoding, and inherently lossy, as the round trip through pixel space discards rich features of the learned latent representation. In this paper, we introduce \emph{latent spatial memory} for video world models, a persistent 3D cache that stores scene information directly in the diffusion latent space, avoiding pixel-space reconstruction. Building on this, we propose Mirage, a latent-space spatial memory framework that constructs the memory by lifting latent tokens into 3D via depth-guided back-projection and queries it by synthesizing novel views through direct latent-space warping. This unified formulation eliminates both the information loss of pixel-space reconstruction and the computational burden of repeated encoding and rendering. Experiments show that latent spatial memory achieves up to \textbf{10.57}$\times$ faster end-to-end video generation and \textbf{55}$\times$ reduction in memory footprint relative to explicit 3D baselines. Leveraging the geometric prior of the diffusion model, Mirage attains state-of-the-art performance on WorldScore and strong reconstruction quality on RealEstate10K.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses latent diffusion for video world models

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes latent spatial memory that stores 3D scene information directly in diffusion latent space, avoiding pixel-space rendering and enabling efficient video generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有视频世界模型在RGB空间显式构建点云记忆，计算昂贵且丢失潜在特征。本文提出潜在空间记忆Mirage，将场景信息直接存储在扩散潜在空间中，通过深度引导反投影构建3D缓存，避免像素空间重建。该方法提升了生成效率并保留了丰富的潜在特征，为生成式视频编码提供了可借鉴的潜在空间操作思路。

quick_skim.title: FOGO: Forgetting-aware Orthogonalization Optimizer
quick_skim.abstract: We argue that forgetting is not confined to continual learning but is a general optimization phenomenon: during standard training, dominant mini-batch gradients suppress rare but useful update directions, causing short-term forgetting at every step. When such knowledge is never revisited, these losses compound into long-term forgetting-the classical failure mode of continual learning. We introduce FOGO, a scalable optimizer that continuously detects and resolves gradient interference across both regimes. FOGO spectrally orthogonalizes momentum updates to prevent dominant directions from monopolizing optimization, then stores representative past directions in a compact codebook memory built on random projection, where pairwise distances are provably preserved in low-dimensional space. At each step, conflicts between the current update and stored directions are resolved via lightweight orthogonal correction and lifted back through a proximal step, with minimal overhead and no data storage. Across class-imbalanced classification, continual visual learning under domain and class shifts, continual fine-tuning of LLaVA-7B, and GPT-2 pretraining, FOGO consistently improves convergence and knowledge retention, outperforming Adam and Muon.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: General optimizer addressing gradient interference during training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通用的训练优化器，解决梯度干扰
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通用的训练优化器，解决梯度干扰
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通用的训练优化器，解决梯度干扰
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes FOGO, a scalable optimizer that spectrally orthogonalizes momentum updates to prevent dominant directions from monopolizing optimization, applicable to various training regimes including neural codec training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文指出遗忘不仅是持续学习的问题，也是标准训练中普遍存在的优化现象：主导梯度抑制了稀有但有用的更新方向。为此提出FOGO优化器，通过谱正交化动量更新来防止主导方向垄断优化，并使用随机投影构建紧凑码本记忆存储历史方向。FOGO可应用于多种训练场景，为提升神经网络训练质量（包括视频压缩模型）提供了有效工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文指出遗忘不仅是持续学习的问题，也是标准训练中普遍存在的优化现象：主导梯度抑制了稀有但有用的更新方向。为此提出FOGO优化器，通过谱正交化动量更新来防止主导方向垄断优化，并使用随机投影构建紧凑码本记忆存储历史方向。FOGO可应用于多种训练场景，为提升神经网络训练质量（包括视频压缩模型）提供了有效工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索视频压缩模型的训练 trick，包括量化、蒸馏、剪枝、多阶段训练等技术，调研不同优化策略对编码性能、推理速度、模型大小的提升效果，设计适用于端侧部署的轻量化训练方案。, especially work that connects or combines: Low-complexity codec; neural video compression model training techniques; quantization aware training for video coding models; knowledge distillation strategies for video compression; model pruning methods for lightweight video codecs; multi-stage training pipeline for neural video compression; Differentiable quantization optimization for latent in video compression; Knowledge distillation for efficient neural video codec training; Model pruning and acceleration for end to end video coding; Multi stage training strategy for rate distortion performance improvement.

quick_skim.title: TENP: Trapezoidal Expert Neuron Pruning For Mixture-of-Experts
quick_skim.abstract: Mixture-of-Experts large language models (LLMs) scale efficiently through sparse activation, yet their deployment is fundamentally constrained by the large static parameter footprint of experts. Existing compression approaches either remove entire experts, disrupting routing topology and harming performance, or rely on unstructured weight pruning with limited practical efficiency. To address the limitations, we propose TENP, a structured Trapezoidal ExpertNeuron Pruning framework. Using a few samples, we identify and retain important experts, while applying expert neuron pruning (ENP) to less important experts, reserving model parameters in a trapezoidal pattern from shallow to deep layers. When evaluating expert importance, we jointly consider both the magnitude of the expert output and its ability to change the direction of the input vector. For ENP, we measure each neuron's projected contribution to the expert output to identify and retain important neurons. We conduct extensive experiments on the Qwen and DeepSeek models. Under a routing expert sparsity of 40% and an average of 63.76% activated expert parameters, the DeepSeek model suffers only a 1-point drop in accuracy compared to the full-parameter model. Moreover, it outperforms the full-parameter model by 10% on code generation tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Structured pruning framework for MoE, transferable to lightweight video codecs

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes TENP, a structured trapezoidal expert neuron pruning framework for MoE, retaining important experts while pruning neurons in less important ones, resulting in trapezoidal parameter patterns.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 混合专家大模型因静态参数庞大而部署困难，现有压缩方法或移除整个专家（破坏路由拓扑）或采用非结构化剪枝（效率低）。本文提出TENP结构化梯形专家神经元剪枝框架，通过少量样本识别重要专家，在次要专家上进行神经元剪枝，保留梯形参数模式。该方法在保持性能的同时显著减少参数量，为视频压缩等领域的轻量化设计提供了可借鉴的结构化剪枝思路。
